name: Biology
description: An introduction to living systems — from cell biology and genetics to evolution, ecology, and the diversity of life on Earth.

modules.title: The Chemistry of Life
modules.objectives: Describe atoms, bonds, and the four macromolecules essential to life.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 2: The Chemistry of Life
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Elements and Atoms
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #1 — That's Why Carbon Is A Tramp
modules.assignment: Build a concept map showing how each of the four macromolecules (carbs, lipids, proteins, nucleic acids) relates to its monomer and main function.

modules.title: Cell Structure & Function
modules.objectives: Compare prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; identify organelles and their roles.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Cell Structures
modules.assignment: Draw and label a eukaryotic cell. For each organelle, write one sentence on its function using an analogy.

modules.title: Cell Membrane & Transport
modules.objectives: Explain the fluid mosaic model; distinguish passive from active transport and osmosis.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 5: Membrane Structure & Function
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Fluid Mosaic Model
modules.assignment: Design an experiment to demonstrate osmosis using potato cubes in different salt solutions. Predict and explain expected results.

modules.title: Cellular Respiration
modules.objectives: Trace the steps of glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, and the electron transport chain; calculate net ATP yield.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Steps of Cellular Respiration
modules.assignment: Create a flow chart of cellular respiration showing inputs, outputs, and ATP produced at each stage.

modules.title: Photosynthesis
modules.objectives: Explain the light-dependent and light-independent reactions; describe the role of chlorophyll.
modules.assignment: Write the overall equation for photosynthesis. Explain what would happen to a plant placed in complete darkness for two weeks.

modules.title: Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis
modules.titleZh: 细胞分裂：有丝分裂与减数分裂
modules.objectives: Describe the stages of mitosis and meiosis; compare their purposes and outcomes.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Mitosis Stages
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #12 — Mitosis vs Meiosis
modules.assignment: Create a table comparing mitosis and meiosis on: purpose, number of divisions, daughter cells produced, and ploidy.

modules.title: Mendelian Genetics
modules.objectives: Apply Mendel's laws to predict offspring ratios; construct and interpret Punnett squares.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Mendelian Genetics
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Monohybrid Punnett Square practice
modules.assignment: Set up and solve 3 monohybrid and 1 dihybrid cross. Calculate phenotype and genotype ratios for each.

modules.title: DNA Structure & Replication
modules.objectives: Describe DNA double helix structure; explain semi-conservative replication step by step.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 14: DNA Structure & Replication
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — DNA Replication
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #10 — DNA Structure & Replication
modules.assignment: Given a template DNA strand, write out the complementary strand. Then show the two daughter strands after replication.

modules.title: Gene Expression: Transcription & Translation
modules.objectives: Trace the central dogma DNA → RNA → Protein; explain codon, anticodon, and the role of ribosomes.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Transcription & Translation
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #11 — DNA, Hot Pockets & Genetics
modules.assignment: Given a DNA sequence, transcribe to mRNA and translate to an amino acid chain using a codon chart.

modules.title: Evolution & Natural Selection
modules.objectives: State Darwin's theory of natural selection; distinguish between types of natural selection.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 18: Evolution & Natural Selection
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Natural Selection
modules.assignment: Describe a real-world example of natural selection (e.g., antibiotic resistance). Explain how each of the four conditions of natural selection applies.

modules.title: Diversity of Life & Classification
modules.objectives: Use binomial nomenclature; classify organisms into the six kingdoms and three domains.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 20: Phylogenies & the History of Life
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Phylogenetic Trees
modules.assignment: Classify 5 organisms (from a provided list) using domain, kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, species.

modules.title: Plant Biology
modules.objectives: Identify plant organ systems and tissues; explain vascular transport and the life cycle of flowering plants.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 30: Plant Form & Physiology
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Plant Structures
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Plant Structures & Functions practice
modules.assignment: Label a diagram of a flower showing all major structures. Explain the role of each in reproduction.

modules.title: Human Body Systems
modules.objectives: Describe the major organ systems and explain how they interact to maintain homeostasis.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 33: Animal Form & Function
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Animal Tissues
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #31 — Your Immune System
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Animal Form & Function practice
modules.assignment: Choose any three organ systems. For each, name the major organs and describe one way it interacts with another system.

modules.title: Ecology & Ecosystems
modules.objectives: Define biotic/abiotic factors, food webs, and energy flow; explain population dynamics and human impact.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 44: Ecology & the Biosphere
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Food Chains & Webs
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Food Chains & Food Webs practice
modules.assignment: Draw a food web for a local ecosystem. Identify the producers, primary consumers, and top predators. Predict what happens if one species is removed.

questions.question: Which macromolecule serves as the primary energy source for cells?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Carbohydrates.

questions.question: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitochondrion.

questions.question: Which type of transport requires no energy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Passive transport (diffusion).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The net ATP yield from one glucose molecule in aerobic respiration is approximately:" is: 30–32 ATP.
questions.explanation: True. 30–32 ATP is correct.

questions.question: In photosynthesis, what molecule absorbs light energy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Chlorophyll.

questions.question: Mitosis results in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Two genetically identical daughter cells.

questions.question: In a monohybrid cross of two heterozygous parents (Aa × Aa), the expected phenotype ratio is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 3:1.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A prokaryotic cell differs from a eukaryotic cell in that it:" is: Has mitochondria.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Lacks a membrane-bound nucleus.

questions.question: Osmosis is defined as:
options: Movement of solutes across a membrane
Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute
questions.answer: Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute.

questions.question: The Krebs cycle takes place in the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitochondrial matrix.

questions.question: Meiosis is necessary for:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sexual reproduction (forming gametes).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Dominant alleles are represented by:" is: Uppercase letters.
questions.explanation: True. Uppercase letters is correct.

questions.question: The light reactions of photosynthesis occur in the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Thylakoid membrane.

questions.question: The Calvin cycle produces:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: G3P (used to build glucose).

questions.question: Enzymes function as biological catalysts by:
options: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions
questions.answer: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which bond holds the two strands of DNA together" is: Ionic bonds.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Hydrogen bonds.

questions.question: DNA replication is described as semi-conservative because:
options: Only half the DNA is copied
Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand
Half the cell's DNA is conserved
questions.answer: Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand.

questions.question: During transcription, DNA is used to produce:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: mRNA.

questions.question: A codon consists of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Three nucleotides on mRNA.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Darwin's theory of natural selection requires all EXCEPT:" is: Intentional adaptation by individuals.
questions.explanation: True. Intentional adaptation by individuals is correct.

questions.question: Binomial nomenclature uses which two classification levels?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Genus and Species.

questions.question: Xylem and phloem are plant tissues responsible for:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Transporting water/minerals and sugars respectively.

questions.question: Homeostasis refers to:
options: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions
questions.answer: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "In an ecological food web, producers are typically:" is: Herbivores.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Plants and photosynthetic organisms.

questions.question: A mutation in a somatic cell:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cannot be passed to offspring.

questions.question: The three domains of life are:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Bacteria, Archaea, Eukarya.

questions.question: Antibiotic resistance in bacteria is an example of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Natural selection in action.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The primary function of the cell membrane is:" is: Regulating what enters and exits the cell.
questions.explanation: True. Regulating what enters and exits the cell is correct.

questions.question: Which process breaks down glucose in the absence of oxygen?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fermentation (anaerobic respiration).

questions.question: The human nervous system consists of:
options: The brain and spinal cord only
The central and peripheral nervous systems
questions.answer: The central and peripheral nervous systems
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The central and peripheral nervous systems.

questions.question: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium requires:
options: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population)
questions.answer: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis" is: Lysosome.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Ribosome.

questions.question: Crossing over during meiosis increases:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Genetic diversity in offspring.

questions.question: An organism with two identical alleles for a trait is called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Homozygous.

questions.question: The 10% rule in ecology states that:
options: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level
questions.answer: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level.

quizQuestions.question: Which element is the backbone of all organic molecules?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Carbon.

quizQuestions.question: Proteins are polymers of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Amino acids.

quizQuestions.question: Lipids are primarily used for:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Energy storage and membrane structure.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which element is the backbone of all organic molecules" is: Carbon.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Carbon is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Which organelle contains the cell's genetic material?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Nucleus.

quizQuestions.question: Ribosomes are sites of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Protein synthesis.

quizQuestions.question: The Golgi apparatus:
options: Modifies and packages proteins for export
quizQuestions.answer: Modifies and packages proteins for export
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Modifies and packages proteins for export.

quizQuestions.question: Which structure is found in plant cells but NOT animal cells?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cell wall and chloroplast.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which organelle contains the cell's genetic material" is: Nucleus.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Nucleus is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The cell membrane is composed of a:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Phospholipid bilayer.

quizQuestions.question: Diffusion moves molecules:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: From high to low concentration.

quizQuestions.question: A cell placed in a hypotonic solution will:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Swell and potentially burst (lyse).

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The cell membrane is composed of a:" is: Phospholipid bilayer.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Phospholipid bilayer is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Diffusion moves molecules:" is: Against the concentration gradient.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: From high to low concentration.

quizQuestions.question: Glycolysis occurs in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cytoplasm.

quizQuestions.question: The overall equation for cellular respiration shows glucose being converted to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: CO₂, water, and ATP.

quizQuestions.question: The electron transport chain is located on the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cristae (inner mitochondrial membrane).

quizQuestions.question: Fermentation is used when:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Oxygen is absent or limited.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Glycolysis occurs in the:" is: Cytoplasm.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Cytoplasm is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The overall equation for photosynthesis shows:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂.

quizQuestions.question: Chlorophyll absorbs light primarily in which colors?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Red and blue.

quizQuestions.question: The light-independent reactions (Calvin cycle) take place in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Stroma of the chloroplast.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The overall equation for photosynthesis shows:" is: CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂ is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which gas do plants absorb for photosynthesis."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Carbon dioxide.

quizQuestions.question: The correct order of mitosis phases is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase.

quizQuestions.question: During anaphase, sister chromatids:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Are pulled to opposite poles.

quizQuestions.question: Meiosis II is most similar to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitosis.

quizQuestions.question: How many haploid cells result from one round of meiosis?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 4.

quizQuestions.question: Cytokinesis refers to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The division of the cytoplasm.

quizQuestions.question: Gregor Mendel conducted his genetics experiments on:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pea plants.

quizQuestions.question: A heterozygous genotype contains:
options: One dominant and one recessive allele
quizQuestions.answer: One dominant and one recessive allele
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: One dominant and one recessive allele.

quizQuestions.question: The law of segregation states that:
options: Alleles for different traits assort independently
Each gamete receives one allele for each trait
quizQuestions.answer: Each gamete receives one allele for each trait
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Each gamete receives one allele for each trait.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Gregor Mendel conducted his genetics experiments on:" is: Pea plants.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Pea plants is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The base pairing rule in DNA is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A-T and G-C.

quizQuestions.question: DNA helicase unwinds the double helix by:
options: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs
quizQuestions.answer: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs.

quizQuestions.question: DNA polymerase adds nucleotides in which direction?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 5' to 3'.

quizQuestions.question: The sugar in DNA is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Deoxyribose.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The base pairing rule in DNA is:" is: A-T and G-C.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. A-T and G-C is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The central dogma of molecular biology is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: DNA → RNA → Protein.

quizQuestions.question: Transcription occurs in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Nucleus.

quizQuestions.question: In mRNA, thymine (T) is replaced by:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Uracil.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The central dogma of molecular biology is:" is: DNA → RNA → Protein.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. DNA → RNA → Protein is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Transcription occurs in the:" is: Ribosome.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Nucleus.

quizQuestions.question: Darwin's book proposing evolution by natural selection was called:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: On the Origin of Species.

quizQuestions.question: Fitness in evolutionary terms means:
options: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully
quizQuestions.answer: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully.

quizQuestions.question: Vestigial structures are:
options: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors
quizQuestions.answer: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors.

quizQuestions.question: Genetic drift has the greatest effect in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Small populations.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Darwin's book proposing evolution by natural selection was called:" is: On the Origin of Species.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. On the Origin of Species is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The correct order of taxonomic hierarchy from broadest to most specific is:
options: Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom, Domain
Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Kingdom, Domain, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Domain, Phylum, Kingdom, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.answer: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.

quizQuestions.question: In Homo sapiens, "sapiens" is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Species.

quizQuestions.question: Archaea are similar to bacteria in that they are both:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Prokaryotes.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The correct order of taxonomic hierarchy from broadest to most specific is:" is: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Xylem transports:
options: Water and minerals from roots upward
quizQuestions.answer: Water and minerals from roots upward
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Water and minerals from roots upward.

quizQuestions.question: Stomata are openings that:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Regulate gas exchange in leaves.

quizQuestions.question: The reproductive structures of a flowering plant are found in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Flower.

quizQuestions.question: Pollination is the transfer of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pollen from stamen to pistil.

quizQuestions.question: Phloem transports:
options: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant
quizQuestions.answer: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant.

quizQuestions.question: The primary function of the respiratory system is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gas exchange (O₂ in, CO₂ out).

quizQuestions.question: The site of gas exchange in the lungs is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Alveoli.

quizQuestions.question: Insulin is produced by the pancreas to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Lower blood glucose levels.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The primary function of the respiratory system is:" is: Gas exchange (O₂ in, CO₂ out).
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Gas exchange (O₂ in, CO₂ out) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Producers in an ecosystem are:
options: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis
quizQuestions.answer: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis.

quizQuestions.question: The 10% energy rule means:
options: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level
quizQuestions.answer: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level.

quizQuestions.question: A niche is best described as:
options: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem
The number of organisms in a habitat
quizQuestions.answer: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem.

quizQuestions.question: Biodiversity is important because:
options: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience
quizQuestions.answer: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Producers in an ecosystem are:" is: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis is correct.
